title: Centaurium serotinum
taxonomy: Centaurium serotinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is commonly known as late flower centaury or late gentian. The plant belongs to the genus Centaurium, which includes several species of gentians. Centaurium serotinum is native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Western Asia. It is a perennial herbaceous plant with small, yellow or white flowers.
uses: Centaurium serotinum, also known as late flower centaury, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Traditionally, it has been used to help manage digestive issues, including diarrhea and indigestion. Some herbal remedies also suggest its use in reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds and fever. The plant's antiseptic and antibacterial properties may also contribute to its ability to aid in wound healing. However, its effectiveness for these purposes has not been extensively scientifically proven.

The active constituents of Centaurium serotinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. intro: Centaurium serotinum, commonly known as late flower or late centaury, is a perennial plant belonging to the Gentianaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for treating digestive issues, wounds, and various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium serotinum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Centaurium serotinum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
